answers: Braille is a tactile writing system using raised dots arranged in cells, allowing blind and visually impaired people to read by touch.
Braille holo ekta touch-based reading system ja raised dots use korey — blind manush era angul diye pore.
Braille uses a grid of 6 dots (2 columns, 3 rows) per cell. Different combinations of raised dots represent letters, numbers, and punctuation.
Braille was invented by Louis Braille in 1824 when he was just 15 years old. He was blind himself and adapted a military night-writing system.
